evidence: GREAT ONE, drama packed, dark, good mystery, acting the best Brits own that sector.
Top of the line series as should be expected from this cast of characters.
the plot seems overcome by the great acting actuall y don't know what the plot was or should of been
confusing plot too many loose threads looked like the writers got side tracked with their own complicated plots.
slow pace of the story line tossing abbreviated forms of the involvement of the agencies which bogged down the story even more.
got boring half way through the first season.
Acting superb writing the best all Brit mystery writers I know or have viewed or read have this quality.
cinematography is definetly done in Masterpiece PBS brilliance keeping with the on going grand tradition.
Love this series. Great work is always its own reward.
Cumberbatch and Freeman do a great job as many of their predecessors have.
could feel as a trope John Keats in this work.
camera work is superb ....well worth a look see.
the acting, the characters the magnificent script is highly worthy of my humble praise.
It was fun to watch but the plot seems overcome by the great acting.

summary: The user has a strong preference for mystery and drama genres, appreciating high-quality production and emotional resonance in storytelling. They tend to favor slower-paced narratives but have mixed feelings about narrative complexity, indicating a desire for engaging plots without excessive complication.
